The contents are noted, but the Applicant's timeline is self-serving and incorrect. My investigation into financial irregularities began in March 2025. The meeting with Mr. Bantjes was a culmination of my efforts to bring these issues to the attention of the trustees, not the beginning of the discovery.

This is denied. The discrepancies were first identified by me and presented to Mr. Bantjes. The Applicant is attempting to recast me as the subject of an investigation that I myself initiated. The irregularities include the R5.4 million "stock adjustment" in SLG and two years of unallocated expenses dumped onto RWW.

This is a deliberate oversimplification. While some transactions were IT-related, my investigation uncovered a far wider pattern of financial misconduct, including revenue hijacking and expense dumping across multiple entities, orchestrated by others.

I deny the characterization of my conduct as "erratic." This is a baseless personal attack intended to discredit me. My focus has been on fulfilling my fiduciary duties by investigating and reporting serious financial misconduct. The pressure of this undertaking, in the face of obstruction and sabotage, has been immense.

This is a gross distortion. The Applicant's unilateral cancellation of all business bank cards on 7 June 2025 was an act of sabotage, not a reasonable inquiry. It occurred shortly after I finalized my reports exposing the fraud, and it was designed to paralyze the companies and prevent me from making legitimate payments to creditors.

This is denied. I was actively investigating the legitimacy of these very transactions. The Applicant demanded invoices for expenses that were part of a fraudulent scheme, placing me in an impossible position. My refusal to blindly produce invoices for suspicious transactions was an act of responsible governance.
Admitted. The Applicant's reckless act of cancelling the cards caused immediate and foreseeable harm to the companies' operations, for which he now seeks to blame me.

This is denied. I refused to be complicit in a cover-up. I was not refusing to "assist"; I was refusing to abandon my investigation and rubber-stamp suspicious transactions. My focus was on protecting the companies from further financial damage.
The annexure is noted. These communications, when viewed in the context of my ongoing fraud investigation and the Applicant's sabotage, will not support his narrative.
The contents of this paragraph are noted.

This is denied. It was not a matter of "would not" or "could not," but rather that I was actively investigating the legitimacy of the underlying transactions. Providing invoices would have validated potentially fraudulent expenses.

This is denied. I instructed staff to preserve data and assist with my investigation into financial misconduct. This was a necessary and proper exercise of my authority as a director to protect the company's interests.

I admit that I restricted access to certain data. This was a critical security measure to prevent the spoliation of evidence by those implicated in the financial irregularities I had uncovered. It was a temporary measure to preserve the integrity of the investigation.
The allegation of "interference" is denied. My actions have been aimed at safeguarding the companies' assets and data.

This is emphatically denied. Investigating and stopping financial misconduct, preserving evidence, and preventing further losses are fundamentally in the best interests of the corporations. The Applicant's position is perverse.
The contents of this paragraph are noted.

The relief sought is denied. This application is a malicious and strategic abuse of process, brought with the ulterior motive of removing me from my position after I uncovered and reported extensive financial misconduct within the corporate entities. The timing of this application, following my reports, is no coincidence.

The allegations are denied. The Applicant's feigned urgency is a fabrication. The true motive is to prevent the further exposure of the very financial irregularities I was investigating. My actions, far from justifying this application, were aimed at protecting the companies from ongoing financial harm.

This allegation is emphatically denied. My conduct was aimed at preventing detriment to the companies. The true detrimental conduct is the systematic financial sabotage I uncovered, including the dumping of two years of unallocated expenses onto RegimA Worldwide (RWW) on 30 March 2025, which I was pressured to sign off on within 12 hours.

This is a bare denial. The Applicant fails to cite a single specific action that constitutes a contravention of my fiduciary duties. To the contrary, my investigation and subsequent reports were a direct fulfillment of my duty to act in the best interests of the companies.

The contents are noted as the Applicant's statement of the law. However, the Applicant has failed to provide any factual basis to meet the requirements of these sections. This application is a textbook example of strategic litigation against a whistleblower.
The contents are noted as a recitation of the law.
The contents are noted as a recitation of the law.

The contents are noted. I have always been acutely aware of my fiduciary duties. It was precisely my adherence to these duties that compelled me to investigate the financial irregularities that form the true basis of this dispute.

The contents are noted. I deny any misconduct. The Applicant is attempting to use the court to disqualify the very person who was acting to protect the companies from the actual misconduct of others.

I deny these allegations in the strongest possible terms. The only "harm" I have inflicted is upon the fraudulent scheme that was operating within the companies. My actions were not "gross abuse" but a necessary and lawful exercise of my duties. The Applicant's claims of fraud against me are a malicious inversion of the truth. The real fraud is the R5.4 million "stock adjustment" in Strategic Logistics (SLG) and the systematic hijacking of revenue streams from RWW, which began on 1 March 2025.

I deny these allegations. The Applicant's claim of "oppressive conduct" is a fabrication. The true oppression was the Applicant and his associates dumping two years of unallocated expenses on RWW and demanding I sign off on them within 12 hours on 30 March 2025. This was a clear attempt to make me complicit in their scheme. My refusal to do so is not oppressive conduct; it is responsible governance.

The Applicant's conclusion is noted. It is based on a distorted and incomplete version of events, and I am advised that it has no prospect of success.

I wholeheartedly agree and consent to an order for a truly independent forensic investigation. Such an investigation will vindicate me and confirm the substance of my reports. The investigation must have a broad mandate to investigate the R5.4 million SLG stock adjustment, the two years of unallocated expenses, the diversion of revenue from RWW starting on 1 March 2025, the secret cancellation of my business bank cards on 7 June 2025, and the role of Villa Via in extracting profits from the so-called "Group".

I agree. This is the central thesis of the reports I compiled between March and June 2025. The Applicant's attempt to attribute this to me is a desperate and transparent attempt to deflect blame from the individuals who are actually responsible for the missing funds. The Applicant has been aware of these issues since I first reported them and has chosen to attack the messenger rather than address the message.
The allegations herein are denied.
